steps.title: Identify the y-intercept location
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis. This occurs when $x = 0$. We need to find the y-coordinate at that location.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph at x = 0
steps.content: Looking at the graph where the line intersects the y-axis (the vertical line at $x = 0$), the line crosses at $y = -4$. Therefore, the y-intercept is the point $(0, -4)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
